essence: Studies ecosystems, wildlife, and habitats to protect biodiversity and guide sustainable land use.

identity.title: Ecologist
identity.summary: Conducts ecological surveys, analyses habitats and species data, prepares environmental reports, and advises on conservation and sustainable development.

salary.notes: Senior ecologists, licensed specialists (e.g., bats, newts), or consultancy leads can exceed £60k-£75k+.

qualifications: Degree or training in ecology, environmental science, or conservation
Knowledge of species identification and habitat assessment
Ability to conduct ecological surveys and impact assessments
Understanding of biodiversity legislation and protected species

lifestyle.description: Ecologists work across natural habitats, development sites, conservation areas, and research projects. They conduct surveys, analyse ecological data, prepare reports, and advise on biodiversity protection. The role suits individuals who enjoy wildlife, fieldwork, scientific analysis, and environmental stewardship.

habits: Advises on conservation and mitigation strategies
Works with planners, developers, and conservation bodies
